name: negotiate_calc
description: Calculates the next counter-offer given the consumer's proposed payment and account balance. Must be called before stating any counter-offer to the consumer. Never invent numbers — always use this tool's response.

request_body_schema.description:
Top-level body description (separate from each property's own description) — one line summarizing the whole payload's purpose:

**`negotiate_calc`**
> "Request to calculate a compliant counter-offer based on the consumer's payment offer, the account balance, and how many negotiation rounds have happened so far."

**`send_outcome`**
> "Final call result to log and route downstream — exactly one outcome type plus the agreed payment details, sent once at call end."

If the form only has one description slot per param (not a separate top-level one) and you're filling it for a specific param right now, tell me which param and which tool and I'll give the exact line for that slot.

consumer_offer.description: Extract the exact payment amount in the account's currency that the consumer just offered to pay. Return the number only, no currency symbol.

attempt_no.description: Track which negotiation round this is. Start at 1 on the first offer/counter-offer exchange. Increment to 2 only if the consumer counters after hearing the first tool-calculated offer. Never exceed 2
